Off Calendar Dec-02-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOSEPH M. BREALL OF BREALL & BREALL, LLP, TEL. NO. 345-0545 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF AND CROSS-DEFENDANT;
ATTY. SCOTT A. FREEDMAN & ATTY. PAUL UTRECHT OF ZACKS & UTRECHT, TEL. NO. 956-8100 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS AND CROSS-COMPLAINANTS.
RULING - JUDGE KAHN MADE A DISCLOSURE REGARDING HIS SON & MR. BREALL'S SON WHO PLAYED BASEBALL TOGETHER. MR. BREALL IS ALSO MARRIED TO A DISTRICT ATTORNEY WHO APPEARED BEFORE HIM AND HIS SISTER IS A SUPERIOR COURT JUDGE AND IS A COLLEAGUE OF HIM. HE DOES NOT VIEW THIS AS A BASIS FOR RECUSAL. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. Cross-defendant Bonnie Cohen's motion for summary judgment or alternatively, for motion for summary adjudication is taken under submission as to Cross-Complainant Ann Gregory. Summary judgment is denied as to Cross-complainants Tina Frank, Robert and Zusana Forrest because it appears that, even if the cross-claims were adjudicated to be without merit, Cohen's own claims against each of the defendants/cross-complainants still remain. Summary adjudication is denied because: 1) Cohen did not comply with the requirements of CRC 3.1350(h) regarding the format of a separate statement in that Cohen's separate statement did not delineate any particular issues; 2) Cohen did not satisfy her burden of production that the 'dog-related' and/or 'furniture in the common areas' issues that have arisen since the execution of the settlement agreement are barred by the settlement agreement; and 3) the remaining issues raised by Cohen, even if her substantive positions on them are correct, do not dispose of an entire claim against any cross-complainant and thus are not amenable to summary adjudication. Each side to prepare form of order for the court's consideration. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 =302/hek

Appearances: Joseph M. Breall of Breall & Breall (345.0545) for plaintiff and responding party; Patrick J. Connolly of Phillips Spallas & Angstadt LLP (278.9400) for defendants Tina Frank, Robert and Zuzana Forrest and Ann Gregory.

Argued and the court took the matter under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
-------------------------------------------------
After hearing the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
Defendant Washington Baker Association's motion to strike portions of plaintiff's second amended complaint is granted. This settlement agreement does not sufficiently affect the vital concerns of the individual such that plaintiff is entitled to emotional and mental damages for Breach of Contract.
